file.content:
# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify
Started: (will be replaced on first run)
---

## Codebase Patterns
*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*

- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql`.
- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.
- **Verify RLS via a transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block:** seed fresh fixtures with known `auth.uid`s, then `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'` and assert row visibility via `insert into _asserts ...` rows that you `select *` at the end before rolling back. Important: `grant insert, select on _asserts to authenticated` before switching roles, else you'll get `permission denied for table _asserts`. `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only — don't use it outside a DO block. Check `information_schema.columns` before writing fixtures so INSERTs match real schema (columns migrate between blocks — e.g. `listing_version_id` lives on `applications`, not `submissions`).
- **Trigger function hardening pattern (mandatory for every new trigger / PL/pgSQL function in this repo):**
  1. Declare `set search_path = ''` on the function (CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses, so restate it on every redefinition).
  2. With empty `search_path`, qualify EVERY user-defined ref: tables `public.<tbl>`, types/enum casts `public.<type>` (e.g. `'tiktok'::public.platform`), `app_private.<fn>` for sibling functions.
  3. For pg_catalog **functions** (`now()`, `hashtextextended()`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock()`, etc.): bare is fine — `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched even with empty `search_path`. Schema-qualifying as `pg_catalog.<fn>()` also works for true functions.
  4. **GREATEST / LEAST / COALESCE / NULLIF are SQL parser constructs, NOT functions.** They CANNOT be schema-qualified (`pg_catalog.greatest()` raises 42883). Always write them bare.
  5. SECURITY DEFINER functions especially require empty `search_path` — without it, a caller-controlled search_path can shadow `public.<table>` and divert writes. The advisor `function_search_path_mutable` flags this at WARN.
- **US-008 TODO — out-of-order Apify webhook delivery:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` to guard the timestamp column, but the surrounding column UPDATEs (e.g. `tiktok_follower_count`) are unconditional. If a webhook with an older `fetched_at` lands after a newer one (different `apify_run_id`, so the dedup index doesn't catch it), the trigger will overwrite fresh denorm columns with stale values even though the timestamp itself refuses to roll back. The spec §15 has the same gap. Resolution when US-008 (apify-core / webhook receiver) lands: either add `where new.fetched_at >= public.creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` to each branch's UPDATE, or guard the whole denorm block on a max(fetched_at) check inside the advisory lock. Update the spec section in the same PR.
- **US-008 nice-to-have — advisory lock collision risk:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses single-arg `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(...))`. Two distinct (social_link_id, scrape_mode) strings can collide to the same 64-bit key, causing unrelated creators to serialize unnecessarily. Not a correctness issue (only throughput). Standard fix is the two-arg form `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))`. Spec §15 codifies the single-arg form; address as a spec amendment when load matters.
- **`public.events` append-only is a social contract, not a DB-level guarantee.** US-008 reproduces the spec literally (`revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated`), but `service_role` and `postgres` retain every grant including UPDATE/DELETE — by design per spec §4.4 lines 1083–1085 ("Service role only. No application read/write policy"). Any edge function or trigger that writes to `events` must treat INSERT as the only safe verb; there is no DB trigger that blocks UPDATE/DELETE. If stronger enforcement is ever desired, add a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger raising `RAISE EXCEPTION` (this would also block maintenance scripts — considered too restrictive for v1).
- **In PL/pgSQL with `set search_path = ''`, `text[] || 'literal'` resolves to `anyarray || anyarray` and crashes with `22P02 malformed array literal`.** Postgres chooses the array+array overload for unknown-type literals. Always cast the right-hand side: `changed_fields || 'price_cents'::text`. Typed text columns (e.g. `new.version_bump_reason`) work bare because the operator resolves to `anyarray || anyelement` when the right side has a concrete element type. This gotcha is independent of the `public.<type>` schema-qualification pattern already in Codebase Patterns. Resolved in US-010 via `us_010_fix_array_append` follow-up migration.
- **US-010 hard dependency for US-038 and US-049-055:** the cascade trigger (`app_private.bump_listing_version`) swaps `listings.current_version_id` to the new version, but it does NOT refresh the cached `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` threshold columns. Those columns are populated by §15b (`app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds` on `listing_conditions` + `app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump` on `listings.current_version_id`) which was intentionally scoped OUT of US-010 per its AC. Before any edge function that calls `app_private.request_listing_version_bump` ships, or the discover feed's eligibility filter (US-038) is wired up, §15b triggers MUST land — otherwise threshold columns silently go stale on every conditions-only bump, the feed's threshold index returns wrong eligibility, and the filter lies. Which story claims §15b is open (earliest candidate US-038; the campaign-creation wizard US-049-055 is the latest safe slot).
- **`pg_net.net.http_post(url := NULL, ...)` is a silent no-op, not an error.** When the URL or any required string is NULL (e.g. a `vault.decrypted_secrets` lookup misses), pg_net returns a request id without raising and `cron.job_run_details` logs the run as `succeeded`. Always wrap pg_net calls inside cron bodies in a PL/pgSQL `do $body$ ... $body$` block that resolves required arguments first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` when any are NULL — that surfaces the misconfiguration in `cron.job_run_details` instead of pretending the job ran. Pattern documented in `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql`.
- **Vault-secret pattern for cron-invoked edge functions:** secrets live in `vault.decrypted_secrets` (named `marketify_<purpose>` so they cluster in the dashboard). Migrations DO NOT create the secrets — the actual key material is set out-of-band via the Supabase dashboard. Migrations document the required secret names in the header comment so the next operator knows what to populate. Pre-population, the cron raises and the failure is visible in `cron.job_run_details`. Required secrets so far: `marketify_project_url`, `marketify_service_role_key` (both for `cron-refresh-metrics`).
- **`pg_cron` job-name idempotency requires a guarded `cron.unschedule`.** `cron.schedule` raises on duplicate jobname; `cron.unschedule(text)` raises when the name is unknown. Always wrap in `do $$ begin if exists (select 1 from cron.job where jobname = '<name>') then perform cron.unschedule('<name>'); end if; end $$;` before re-scheduling. There is no upsert in the pg_cron 1.6 API.
- **Spec gap (§16 jobs not yet scheduled):** US-011 only schedules `mark-metrics-stale` and `cron-refresh-metrics` (per its AC). §16 also defines `fail-stuck-refreshing` (operationally important — backstops lost Apify webhooks, referenced by §5.2's overlap-guard guarantee), `prune-read-notifications`, `prune-signup-attempts`, `prune-expired-revocations`. None has an assigned story. `fail-stuck-refreshing` should land alongside the webhook receiver story (US-016+); the prune jobs can land with their respective table stories (`signup_attempts` + `revoked_sessions` tables don't exist yet — they belong with the JWT/session story group).
- **Spec gap (§16 staleness marker leaves `is_latest=false, status='fresh'` rows untouched):** the spec's UPDATE filters on `is_latest`, so any snapshot that was demoted by `denorm_metrics` (which sets `is_latest=false` but does NOT change `status`) and is also old will accumulate forever as `fresh`-but-orphaned. UI is unaffected (it reads `is_latest=true`), but storage hygiene degrades. Resolution: either drop the `is_latest` filter from the cron, or have `denorm_metrics` also set the demoted row's `status` to `stale`. Spec amendment, not a story-blocking fix.
- **Edge functions are Deno, not RN — exclude `supabase/` from the Expo toolchain.** tsc (`tsconfig.json` exclude) and eslint (`eslint.config.js` ignores) both skip `supabase/**`. Edge-function code imports `Deno.env`, uses Web Crypto, and runs under Supabase Edge Runtime. Caveat: tsc `exclude` does not prevent files pulled in via imports, so app code must never import from `supabase/functions/*`. Any cross-boundary types should be re-declared or lifted to `src/types/`.
- **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key.
- **CORS headers shared module (`supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts`):** `corsHeaders` allows `*` origin — safe because edge functions authenticate via `Authorization: Bearer <marketify_jwt>`, not cookies. Every edge function must splat this into responses and handle an `OPTIONS` pre-flight with `{ status: 204, headers: corsHeaders }`.
- **Atomic multi-insert edge-function flow uses a PL/pgSQL RPC, not back-to-back supabase-js inserts.** Two `supabase.rpc`/`.insert()` calls are two separate PostgREST transactions, so a mid-flight crash leaves orphan rows. The established pattern (US-014) is a single `public.<flow>_rpc` PL/pgSQL function invoked via `supabase.rpc(...)` from the edge function — PL/pgSQL wraps the body in an implicit transaction. Catch `unique_violation` with `GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS v_constraint = constraint_name` and return `{error: CODE}` jsonb; let everything else `RAISE` so the edge function can log+500. Implicit-constraint names (`<table>_<column>_key`) are Postgres defaults — ALWAYS comment the expected names in the exception block, since a future migration that renames them would silently downgrade 409s to 500s. Revoke execute from anon/authenticated/public; grant only to service_role (no SECURITY DEFINER needed — service_role already has BYPASSRLS).
- **Supabase edge-function secrets are set via `supabase secrets set --env-file <tmpfile> --project-ref <ref>`, not via Supabase MCP.** The MCP has no secrets tool. Create a temp file containing `NAME=value`, run the CLI, delete the temp file. Never echo the secret value into chat. After setting, re-invoke the edge function — secret names are available via `Deno.env.get(...)`. Running the edge function before the secret is set surfaces as a 500 at the point the helper tries to read it (for `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`, that's `signJwt` → `"MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET is not set"` → caught → returned as `{error: "JWT_ERROR"}` in the current auth-* pattern).
- **Edge-function smoke tests live under `scripts/test/<fn-name>.ts`, invoked with `bun run`.** Read `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` from `process.env` (bun auto-loads `.env`). Send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers even for `verify_jwt=false` functions — the Supabase gateway still requires a valid apikey at the edge. Use `Date.now()` for unique test fixture values so repeat runs don't collide. Every conflict branch (e.g. 409 USERNAME_TAKEN vs 409 EMAIL_TAKEN) gets its own case — the constraint-name mapping in the RPC's exception handler is only validated by cases that actually hit each branch. Clean up test rows via `execute_sql` after each run.
- **Anonymous `unique` declarations in CREATE TABLE get Postgres default names (`<table>_<column>_key`)** — e.g. `username citext not null unique` on `public.users` produces `users_username_key`. Any PL/pgSQL that inspects `constraint_name` depends on this. Verify via `select conname from pg_constraint where conrelid = 'public.<table>'::regclass` before hardcoding a name. If you later want to rename, drop+re-add with explicit `constraint <name> unique (col)` — and update every dependent exception handler in the same migration.
- **Apify REST endpoint + slug encoding:** use `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<rest-slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_KEY>&waitForFinish=<secs>`. REST slugs replace `/` with `~` (`clockworks~tiktok-scraper`, `apify~instagram-scraper`). `waitForFinish` makes Apify block up to N seconds before returning the run object — if the run exceeds N, Apify returns `status=RUNNING` and webhooks pick up the terminal state later. Response shape is `{ data: { id, defaultDatasetId, status, ... } }`. For this project the wrapper at `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` uses direct REST (not the `apify-client` SDK) because Deno in Edge Runtime handles plain fetch cleanly and `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` is the sanctioned way to read the token.
- **Never echo external API error bodies into thrown Error strings when the request URL carries a secret in the query string.** Apify (and several other providers) sometimes include the request URL in error payloads, so `throw new Error(\`... ${body}\`)` can land the token in edge-function logs. Throw a fixed-string error with status + slug only; if you need the body for debugging, log it separately with redaction.
- **Deno tests for `_shared/` modules live alongside them as `<name>.test.ts` and run with `deno test --allow-env supabase/functions/_shared/<name>.test.ts`.** Assertions come from `jsr:@std/assert@1`. Mock fetch by replacing `globalThis.fetch` with a spy that records `{url, init}` and returns a canned `Response`; always restore in a `try/finally`. Deno isn't on every dev machine; `brew install deno` on macOS is a one-liner. If Deno isn't available in a given iteration, document it in the progress entry and have the CI for this run the tests.
- **Public async wrappers must be declared `async` if they do any synchronous validation that might throw**, so the throw surfaces as a Promise rejection rather than a synchronous exception. `assertRejects` in `@std/assert` fails with "Function throws when expected to reject" if the function throws synchronously — a real footgun that can only be caught by actually running the test.

file.content:
- Spec gap (story vs spec naming): story AC said "metrics-refresh edge function"; spec §5.2 distinguishes the user-facing `metrics-refresh` (US-021) from the internal `cron-refresh-metrics`. Followed spec naming; the cron deliberately points at the internal function (different rate-limit + auth shape).
- Spec gap (§16 unscheduled jobs): see Codebase Patterns. `fail-stuck-refreshing` is the most operationally important — should land with the webhook receiver story group (US-016+).
- Spec gap (staleness marker orphan rows): see Codebase Patterns. UI unaffected.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - pg_net's silent-success-on-NULL is the kind of footgun where the obvious code path looks fine but the failure mode is invisible. When a cron body depends on an external lookup (vault, dynamic config, etc.), wrap it in PL/pgSQL with explicit NULL handling — never inline the lookup as a sub-select directly in `net.http_post(url := ...)`.
  - `pg_cron` has no upsert; the `do $$ ... if exists ... then perform cron.unschedule(...) ...` pattern is the standard idempotency wrapper. Save it for any future cron migration.
  - `create extension pg_net with schema extensions` is accepted but pg_net always installs functions in the `net` schema regardless of WITH SCHEMA. Document this in the migration header so the next reader doesn't go hunting in `extensions.net.http_post`.
  - When the reviewer flags a spec-level gap (not a code bug), promote it to Codebase Patterns rather than fixing it in the current story. The one-story-per-iteration rule trumps "while we're here".
---

All three metric_snapshots rows `is_latest=true` (one per scrape_mode). listing.current_version_id wired to v1 without re-triggering the US-010 cascade (the follow-up UPDATE touches only current_version_id — no versioned-field diff).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417170000_us_012_seed.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` unchanged (seed is pure DML — no DDL).
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One cosmetic note (`is_verified=true` on the ig_details row is silently discarded by the denorm trigger since `instagram_is_verified` is not a modeled denorm column — stored in snapshot but harmless; not worth a follow-up migration given append-only rules).
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Fixed UUIDs for seeded rows (`11111111-1111-1111-1111-1111111110XX` pattern) let later stories reference the seed without having to query-by-handle first. Later stories that rely on the seed (US-038 discover feed filter, US-055 edit-campaign cascade verification, US-057 override-eligibility approval) should import these IDs as constants rather than re-querying.
  - Safe pattern for seeding an `active` listing without triggering the US-010 cascade: (a) INSERT listing with `status='active'` + `current_version_id=NULL`, (b) INSERT listing_versions v1, (c) UPDATE only `current_version_id` on the listing. The UPDATE fires `trg_bump_listing_version` but `price_cents`/`currency`/`max_submissions` are unchanged and `version_bump_reason` is NULL, so `versioned_changed=false` and no cascade runs. Avoids the alternative of disabling/re-enabling the trigger mid-migration.
  - `denorm_metrics` only mirrors columns that exist on `creator_profiles`. Extra columns on the snapshot (e.g. `is_verified` on `ig_details`) are legal but silently ignored — no Instagram verification denorm column exists per spec §4.7. Don't fabricate values for snapshot columns that don't map anywhere.

## 2026-04-17 17:45 — US-013
- Added `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (HS256 sign/verify via Web Crypto) and `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (shared `corsHeaders` const). JWT claim shape is `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 though the story AC listed only 4 claims; spec wins.
- `verifyJwt` rejects non-HS256 `alg`, missing claims, `exp ≤ now`, and `iat > now + 60s`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` with a `["verify"]`-only key (not re-sign + constant-time compare) per reviewer feedback. Base64url decode wraps `atob` in try/catch so malformed inputs surface as `"Malformed JWT"` rather than DOMExceptions.
- Excluded `supabase/**` from the Expo toolchain: `tsconfig.json` now has `"exclude": ["node_modules", "supabase/**/*"]`; `eslint.config.js` ignores the same glob. Edge functions run in Deno and import `Deno.env`, so they belong outside the RN typecheck. Added `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET=` to `.env.example` (secret to be populated out-of-band).
- Files changed: `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (new), `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (new), `tsconfig.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `.env.example`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no DB changes) / mobile-mcp n/a (no UI)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Important: (1) `verifyJwt` rewritten to use `crypto.subtle.verify` with verify-only key, eliminating the dual-use key smell; (2) base64 decode + JSON parses wrapped so malformed JWTs raise `"Malformed JWT"` consistently).
- Spec gap: story AC lists `{sub, role, iat, exp}` as claims; spec §4.4 lists `{sub, role, session_id}`. Included all five — `session_id` is load-bearing for §15c revocation. Flagged in commit reasoning so future iterations don't "simplify" it away.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Web Crypto's `crypto.subtle.verify` is the preferred verify path — it's constant-time by construction and lets you import the key with minimal `["verify"]` usage. Only reach for manual `sign`-then-constant-time-compare when the API you need (e.g. key fingerprints) isn't available.
  - `atob` throws DOMException on malformed base64, not a JS Error. Any edge-function helper that decodes untrusted input must wrap `atob` in try/catch and rethrow a normalized Error so callers can uniformly `.catch(e => e.message)`.
  - The tsc `exclude` pattern does NOT stop files from being pulled in via transitive imports; it only filters the project-file glob. For runtime-split code (RN vs Deno), the exclusion holds only as long as app code never imports from the excluded tree. Add a comment on the boundary if this becomes a recurring temptation.
  - When an acceptance criterion and the authoritative spec disagree on claim shape (or any other contract detail), the spec wins per Ralph rules — but note the deviation in both the commit reasoning and Codebase Patterns so the next iteration doesn't try to reconcile back toward the story text.

352-### 3g. Webhook Security
353-
354:- We attach `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret: <token>` via `headersTemplate` on every webhook registration. Edge Function rejects requests without a matching secret; compare via `crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual`-equivalent (constant-time).
355-- Webhook endpoint is idempotent: `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id` has a partial unique index, so redelivery of the same `run_id` no-ops on the second write.
356-- Webhook event types registered: `ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED`, `ACTOR.RUN.FAILED`, `ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT`, `ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED`. All four resolve the snapshot (either `fresh` or `failed`).
357-
358-**Secret rotation procedure (dual-secret window):**
359:1. Edge function accepts EITHER `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` (current) OR `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` (grace). Both env vars are read and compared in constant time; either match succeeds.
360:2. Set `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS = <current>`, then generate a new random 32-byte token and set `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET = <new>`.
361-3. New Apify runs registered after this change carry the new secret in their `headersTemplate`. In-flight runs (registered with the previous secret) continue to deliver successfully because the edge function still accepts the previous secret.
362:4. After the longest possible run duration (we set `timeoutSecs: 120` + Apify processing ≤ 10 min buffer = **15 minutes**), the janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` will have fail-closed any surviving snapshots. Unset `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` to complete rotation.

2-
3-**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is separate (see `design.md`).
4:**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + **Apify** (two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`, `apify/instagram-scraper`).
5-**Posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, a default is chosen with justification.
6-
--
63-|---|---|
64-| Rate limits | Apify costs real money per scrape. Server-side throttle: manual refresh once per 6h per `(user, platform)`. Background refresh daily via `pg_cron` for all linked handles with `fetched_at < now() - 24h`. |
65:| Scrape failures | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh \| stale \| refreshing \| failed`. "Outdated" badge if `fetched_at > 7d`. After 3 consecutive failed scrapes, `social_link.status = failed_fetch`, prompt creator to re-enter handle. |
66-| Video integrity | Validate via TikTok/IG oEmbed at submission time (`422` if 404). Nightly sweep re-validates `approved` submissions — flags but doesn't change state. |
67-| Post-submission performance | Out of MVP. Later: an Apify actor per submitted video to pull current view count. |
--
150-**Sources:**
151-- `apify-client` NPM package — [github.com/apify/apify-client-js](https://github.com/apify/apify-client-js)
152:- TikTok Scraper actor page — [apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper](https://apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper)
153-- Instagram Scraper actor page — [apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper](https://apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper)
154-
--
159-| Platform | Actor Slug | REST slug (with `~`) | Purpose |
160-|---|---|---|---|
161:| TikTok | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` | `clockworks~tiktok-scraper` | Profile + last N videos in a single run |
162-| Instagram | `apify/instagram-scraper` | `apify~instagram-scraper` | Profile details **and** posts/reels — two runs per refresh (different `resultsType`) |
163-
--
169-In practice we use the `apify-client` JS SDK (`.actor(slug).call()` for sync, `.actor(slug).start()` for async) — it wraps both endpoints.
170-
171:### 3b. TikTok — `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`
172-
173-**Input (profile scrape):**
--
195-|---|---|---|
196-| `authorMeta.name` | handle | verification vs. input |
197:| `authorMeta.fans` | follower count | `follower_count` |
198-| `authorMeta.following` | following count | (stored for profile, not eligibility) |
199-| `authorMeta.heart` | total lifetime likes across videos | (stored) |
--
207-
208-**Metric derivation (TikTok):**
209:- `follower_count = items[0].authorMeta.fans`
210-- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(items[i].playCount for i in 0..min(10, len(items)))`
211-- `video_count = items[0].authorMeta.video`
--
232-|---|---|---|
233-| `username` | handle | verification |
234:| `followersCount` | follower count | `follower_count` |
235-| `followsCount` | following count | (stored) |
236-| `postsCount` | total posts | `media_count` |
--
263-
264-**Metric derivation (Instagram):**
265:- `follower_count = details[0].followersCount`
266-- `media_count = details[0].postsCount`
267-- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(p.videoPlayCount ?? p.videoViewCount for p in posts where p.type === "Video")` — skip the profile entirely if there are fewer than 1 video posts (set `avg_views_last_10 = null`, surface in UI as "Not enough video posts to compute").
--
279-// --- synchronous short-run (used on first signup — user is waiting) ---
280-const tiktok = await apify
281:  .actor('clockworks/tiktok-scraper')
282-  .call(
283-    {
--
309-        payloadTemplate: JSON.stringify({
310-          social_link_id: '<id>',
311:          scrape_mode: 'ig_details',       // or 'ig_posts' | 'tiktok_profile'
312-          run_id: '{{resource.id}}',
313-          status: '{{resource.status}}',
--
330-| Denormalized field on `creator_profiles` | TikTok source | Instagram source |
331-|---|---|---|
332:| `tiktok_follower_count` | `authorMeta.fans` | — |
333-| `tiktok_avg_views_last_10` | `mean(playCount[:10])` | — |
334-| `tiktok_video_count` | `authorMeta.video` | — |
335-| `tiktok_is_verified` | `authorMeta.verified` | — |
336:| `instagram_follower_count` | — | `details[0].followersCount` |
337-| `instagram_avg_views_last_10` | — | `mean(videoPlayCount ?? videoViewCount)` across `type === "Video"` posts |
338-| `instagram_media_count` | — | `details[0].postsCount` |
339-| `instagram_is_verified` | — | `details[0].verified` *(reserved; column add in v1.1)* |
340-
341:Stored fully in `metric_snapshots.raw_payload` (jsonb) for forensics and future backfills — if we later need `diggCount` trends we don't need to re-scrape.
342-
343-### 3f. Cost & Rate Management
--
346-- **Scale math (back-of-envelope):** 10k creators × daily cron refresh ≈ $40/day TikTok-only, $200/day IG-only, $240/day fully-linked. That's ~$7.2k/month worst case. Sensitivity: cutting daily cron to every 48h halves that; capping free-tier creators to weekly refresh quarters it.
347-- **Hard throttle:** `metrics/refresh` server-side limit 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)`. `429` returned before any Apify call.
348:[Omitted long matching line]
349-- Set Apify run timeout (via `timeoutSecs` input to `.start()`) to 120s; if actor hangs, we fail-close the snapshot (`status=failed`) and retry on next cron window.
350-- Memory: default `memoryMbytes` is fine for both actors (1024 MB); don't override.
--
353-
354-- We attach `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret: <token>` via `headersTemplate` on every webhook registration. Edge Function rejects requests without a matching secret; compare via `crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual`-equivalent (constant-time).
355:- Webhook endpoint is idempotent: `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id` has a partial unique index, so redelivery of the same `run_id` no-ops on the second write.
356-- Webhook event types registered: `ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED`, `ACTOR.RUN.FAILED`, `ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT`, `ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED`. All four resolve the snapshot (either `fresh` or `failed`).

--
383-| `lister_profiles` | 1:1 extension for lister fields. |
384-| `social_links` | Creator's linked handles (TikTok and/or IG). No tokens — we don't OAuth. |
385:| `metric_snapshots` | Historized metric data per social_link, one Apify run per row. |
386-| `listings` | Core campaign row; mutable fields. |
387-| `listing_versions` | Immutable versioned snapshots. |
--
411-create type metric_status as enum ('fresh', 'stale', 'refreshing', 'failed');
412--- Which Apify actor run produced this snapshot. TikTok has one mode; IG has two (details + posts).
413:create type scrape_mode as enum ('tiktok_profile', 'ig_details', 'ig_posts');
414-create type condition_kind as enum ('pre', 'post');
415-create type condition_metric as enum (
--
429-### 4.3 Denormalization
430-
431:- `creator_profiles.tiktok_follower_count`, `instagram_follower_count`, `_avg_views_last_10`, `metrics_fetched_at` — denormalized from latest `metric_snapshots` via trigger. Enables O(log n) feed filtering.
432-- `listings.current_version_id` — FK to `listing_versions`, avoids feed join.
433-- `listings.approved_submissions_count`, `active_pending_applications_count` — trigger-maintained counters.
--
457-|---|---|
458-| `bump_listing_version()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` — if versioned field changed, INSERT new version, cascade-cancel pending apps, emit notifications. |
459:| `denorm_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — updates `creator_profiles`. |
460-| `submission_side_effects()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status → `approved` — increments count. (Auto-close-on-max deferred to v1.1.) |
461-| `emit_event()` | Helper called by state-changing functions. |
--

content.content:
560	-- History of unlinked rows is retained for audit; an admin override can claim a
561	-- handle currently held by an unlinked row (no extra logic required — the partial
562	-- index permits it).
563	
564	-- =========================================================
565	-- 6. metric_snapshots (historized, one per Apify run)
566	-- =========================================================
567	-- TikTok: 1 row per refresh (scrape_mode='tiktok_profile' — contains followers + avg views).
568	-- Instagram: 2 rows per refresh (scrape_mode='ig_details' for followers, scrape_mode='ig_posts' for avg views).
569	-- The denorm trigger updates only the columns owned by each scrape_mode.
570	create table public.metric_snapshots (
571	  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
572	  social_link_id    uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
573	  scrape_mode       scrape_mode not null,
574	  apify_run_id      text,                  -- dedup across webhook retries
575	  status            metric_status not null,
576	  follower_count    integer,               -- populated on tiktok_profile + ig_details
577	  following_count   integer,               -- populated on tiktok_profile + ig_details
578	  total_likes       bigint,                -- tiktok_profile only
579	  video_count       integer,               -- tiktok_profile (authorMeta.video) / ig_details (postsCount)
580	  avg_views_last_10 integer,               -- tiktok_profile + ig_posts
581	  is_verified       boolean,               -- tiktok_profile + ig_details
582	  raw_payload       jsonb,                 -- full Apify dataset item(s) for forensics
583	  fetched_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
584	  is_latest         boolean not null default false,   -- promoted by trigger under lock
585	  error_message     text
586	  -- scrape_mode ↔ social_link.platform coherence enforced by trg_metric_snapshots_coherence below.
587	);
588	create index on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, fetched_at desc);
589	-- Only one "latest" snapshot per (social_link, scrape_mode). So IG has at most two latest rows.
590	create unique index metric_snapshots_latest
591	  on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, scrape_mode) where is_latest;
592	create unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq
593	  on public.metric_snapshots (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null;
594	-- Janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` scans for rows stuck in 'refreshing' past the
595	-- 10-min timeout. Partial index keeps this sub-millisecond even with millions of rows.
596	create index metric_snapshots_stuck_idx
597	  on public.metric_snapshots (fetched_at) where status = 'refreshing';
598	
599	-- =========================================================
600	-- 7. listings + versioning
601	-- =========================================================
602	create table public.listings (
603	  id                                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
604	  lister_id                         uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete restrict,
605	  status                            listing_status not null default 'draft',
606	  title                             text not null,
607	  description                       text,
608	  -- Feed taxonomy. Enum-ish but stored as text for MVP flexibility; canonical list
609	  -- enforced at edge-function layer against `app_private.listing_categories` view.
610	  -- Used by the feed filter, by design §5.3's "Find a similar bounty" CTA, and by
611	  -- the creator notify-me-when-live toggle (§5.5). v1: 'beauty','fashion','fitness',
612	  -- 'food','tech','gaming','lifestyle','travel','finance','education','music','other'.
613	  category                          text not null default 'other',
614	  price_cents                       integer not null check (price_cents >= 0),
615	  currency                          text not null default 'USD',
616	  max_submissions                   integer,
617	  approved_submissions_count        integer not null default 0,
618	  active_pending_applications_count integer not null default 0,
619	  end_date                          timestamptz,
620	  current_version_id                uuid,
621	  version_number                    integer not null default 1,
622	  -- Cached thresholds for O(log n) feed filtering. Trigger-maintained by
623	  -- app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds(listing_id) whenever the current
624	  -- listing_version's pre-condition rows change.
625	  min_followers_tiktok              integer,
626	  min_followers_instagram           integer,
627	  -- Ephemeral cue consumed by trg_bump_listing_version; NULL in steady state.
628	  -- Values: 'conditions' | 'sample_videos'. Scalar edits (price/max_submissions/currency)
629	  -- are detected directly in the trigger from old.* vs new.*.
630	  version_bump_reason               text,
631	  created_at                        timestamptz not null default now(),
632	  updated_at                        timestamptz not null default now(),
633	  published_at                      timestamptz,
634	  closed_at                         timestamptz
635	);
636	create index listings_feed_idx
637	  on public.listings (status, created_at desc) where status = 'active';
638	create index listings_lister_idx on public.listings (lister_id, status);
639	create index listings_tt_threshold_idx on public.listings (min_followers_tiktok) where status = 'active';
640	create index listings_ig_threshold_idx on public.listings (min_followers_instagram) where status = 'active';
641	-- Feed "find a similar bounty" filter (design §5.3): category + price band.
642	create index listings_category_price_idx
643	  on public.listings (category, price_cents) where status = 'active';
644	
645	create table public.listing_versions (
646	  id                  uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
647	  listing_id          uuid not null references public.listings(id) on delete cascade,
648	  version_number      integer not null,
649	  price_cents         integer not null,
650	  currency            text not null,
651	  max_submissions     integer,
652	  snapshot            jsonb not null,
653	  previous_version_id uuid references public.listing_versions(id),  -- for diff rendering
654	  changed_fields      text[] not null default '{}',                 -- e.g. {price_cents, conditions}
655	  created_at          timestamptz not null default now(),
656	  unique (listing_id, version_number)
657	);
658	create index on public.listing_versions (listing_id, version_number desc);
659	alter table public.listings
660	  add constraint listings_current_version_fk
661	  foreign key (current_version_id) references public.listing_versions(id);
662	
663	-- =========================================================
664	-- 8. listing_conditions (data-driven)
665	-- =========================================================
666	create table public.listing_conditions (
667	  id                 uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
668	  listing_version_id uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
669	  kind               condition_kind not null,
670	  metric             condition_metric not null,
671	  platform           platform,
672	  operator           text not null default 'gte' check (operator in ('gte','lte','eq','contains','bool')),
673	  numeric_threshold  numeric,
674	  text_threshold     text,
675	  bool_threshold     boolean,
676	  created_at         timestamptz not null default now()
677	);
678	create index on public.listing_conditions (listing_version_id, kind);
679

-- =========================================================
-- TikTok: 1 row per refresh (scrape_mode='tiktok_profile' — contains followers + avg views).
-- Instagram: 2 rows per refresh (scrape_mode='ig_details' for followers, scrape_mode='ig_posts' for avg views).
-- The denorm trigger updates only the columns owned by each scrape_mode.
create table public.metric_snapshots (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  social_link_id    uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
  scrape_mode       scrape_mode not null,
  apify_run_id      text,                  -- dedup across webhook retries
  status            metric_status not null,
  follower_count    integer,               -- populated on tiktok_profile + ig_details
  following_count   integer,               -- populated on tiktok_profile + ig_details
  total_likes       bigint,                -- tiktok_profile only
  video_count       integer,               -- tiktok_profile (authorMeta.video) / ig_details (postsCount)
  avg_views_last_10 integer,               -- tiktok_profile + ig_posts
  is_verified       boolean,               -- tiktok_profile + ig_details
  raw_payload       jsonb,                 -- full Apify dataset item(s) for forensics
  fetched_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  is_latest         boolean not null default false,   -- promoted by trigger under lock
  error_message     text
  -- scrape_mode ↔ social_link.platform coherence enforced by trg_metric_snapshots_coherence below.
);
create index on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, fetched_at desc);
-- Only one "latest" snapshot per (social_link, scrape_mode). So IG has at most two latest rows.
create unique index metric_snapshots_latest
  on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, scrape_mode) where is_latest;
create unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq
  on public.metric_snapshots (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null;
-- Janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` scans for rows stuck in 'refreshing' past the
-- 10-min timeout. Partial index keeps this sub-millisecond even with millions of rows.
create index metric_snapshots_stuck_idx
  on public.metric_snapshots (fetched_at) where status = 'refreshing';

-- =========================================================
-- 7. listings + versioning
-- =========================================================
create table public.listings (
  id                                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  lister_id                         uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete restrict,
  status                            listing_status not null default 'draft',
  title                             text not null,
  description                       text,
  -- Feed taxonomy. Enum-ish but stored as text for MVP flexibility; canonical list
  -- enforced at edge-function layer against `app_private.listing_categories` view.
  -- Used by the feed filter, by design §5.3's "Find a similar bounty" CTA, and by
  -- the creator notify-me-when-live toggle (§5.5). v1: 'beauty','fashion','fitness',
  -- 'food','tech','gaming','lifestyle','travel','finance','education','music','other'.
  category                          text not null default 'other',
  price_cents                       integer not null check (price_cents >= 0),
  currency                          text not null default 'USD',
  max_submissions                   integer,
  approved_submissions_count        integer not null default 0,
  active_pending_applications_count integer not null default 0,
  end_date                          timestamptz,
  current_version_id                uuid,
  version_number                    integer not null default 1,
  -- Cached thresholds for O(log n) feed filtering. Trigger-maintained by
  -- app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds(listing_id) whenever the current
  -- listing_version's pre-condition rows change.
  min_followers_tiktok              integer,
  min_followers_instagram           integer,
  -- Ephemeral cue consumed by trg_bump_listing_version; NULL in steady state.
  -- Values: 'conditions' | 'sample_videos'. Scalar edits (price/max_submissions/currency)
  -- are detected directly in the trigger from old.* vs new.*.
  version_bump_reason               text,
  created_at                        timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at                        timestamptz not null default now(),
  published_at                      timestamptz,
  closed_at                         timestamptz
);
create index listings_feed_idx
  on public.listings (status, created_at desc) where status = 'active';
create index listings_lister_idx on public.listings (lister_id, status);
create index listings_tt_threshold_idx on public.listings (min_followers_tiktok) where status = 'active';
create index listings_ig_threshold_idx on public.listings (min_followers_instagram) where status = 'active';
-- Feed "find a similar bounty" filter (design §5.3): category + price band.
create index listings_category_price_idx
  on public.listings (category, price_cents) where status = 'active';

create table public.listing_versions (
  id                  uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_id          uuid not null references public.listings(id) on delete cascade,
  version_number      integer not null,
  price_cents         integer not null,
  currency            text not null,
  max_submissions     integer,
  snapshot            jsonb not null,
  previous_version_id uuid references public.listing_versions(id),  -- for diff rendering
  changed_fields      text[] not null default '{}',                 -- e.g. {price_cents, conditions}
  created_at          timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (listing_id, version_number)
);
create index on public.listing_versions (listing_id, version_number desc);
alter table public.listings
  add constraint listings_current_version_fk
  foreign key (current_version_id) references public.listing_versions(id);

-- =========================================================
-- 8. listing_conditions (data-driven)
-- =========================================================
create table public.listing_conditions (
  id                 uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_version_id uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
  kind               condition_kind not null,
  metric             condition_metric not null,
  platform           platform,
  operator           text not null default 'gte' check (operator in ('gte','lte','eq','contains','bool')),
  numeric_threshold  numeric,
  text_threshold     text,
  bool_threshold     boolean,
  created_at         timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index on public.listing_conditions (listing_version_id, kind);

1	// Shared CORS headers for every Marketify edge function.
2	//
3	// Contract: exports `corsHeaders` (Record<string, string>) to splat into
4	//           every edge function's response and a pre-flight short-circuit.
5	// Auth:     n/a — the browser reads these before the request runs.
6	//
7	// `*` origin is safe here: edge functions authenticate via the
8	// Authorization: Bearer <marketify_jwt> header, not cookies, so there is no
9	// ambient-credential risk to guard against with a narrower origin allowlist.

11	export const corsHeaders: Record<string, string> = {
12	  "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
13	  "Access-Control-Allow-Headers":
14	    "authorization, x-client-info, apikey, content-type",
15	  "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS",
16	};

186	
187	Notes:
188	- `profileSorting: "latest"` returns newest videos first — required so `avg_views_last_10` is the **recent** trailing window, not all-time popular.
189	- `excludePinnedPosts: true` avoids skewing the average with a single mega-viral pinned post.
190	- Download flags default to `true` on Apify; we override to `false` to cut run cost (we never need the media bytes).

193	
194	| Path | Meaning | Maps to |
195	|---|---|---|
196	| `authorMeta.name` | handle | verification vs. input |
197	| `authorMeta.fans` | follower count | `follower_count` |
198	| `authorMeta.following` | following count | (stored for profile, not eligibility) |

file.content:
**Input (profile scrape):**
```json
{
  "profiles": ["<handle>"],
  "resultsPerPage": 10,
  "profileScrapeSections": ["videos"],
  "profileSorting": "latest",
  "excludePinnedPosts": true,
  "shouldDownloadVideos": false,
  "shouldDownloadCovers": false,
  "shouldDownloadSubtitles": false
}
```

Notes:
- `profileSorting: "latest"` returns newest videos first — required so `avg_views_last_10` is the **recent** trailing window, not all-time popular.
- `excludePinnedPosts: true` avoids skewing the average with a single mega-viral pinned post.
- Download flags default to `true` on Apify; we override to `false` to cut run cost (we never need the media bytes).

**Output** — one row per video, with the author profile **denormalized onto every row**. Relevant fields:

| Path | Meaning | Maps to |
|---|---|---|
| `authorMeta.name` | handle | verification vs. input |
| `authorMeta.fans` | follower count | `follower_count` |
| `authorMeta.following` | following count | (stored for profile, not eligibility) |
| `authorMeta.heart` | total lifetime likes across videos | (stored) |
| `authorMeta.video` | total post count | `video_count` |
| `authorMeta.verified` | blue check | `is_verified` |
| `playCount` | **view count for this video** | contributes to `avg_views_last_10` |
| `diggCount` | like count for this video | (stored) |
| `shareCount`, `commentCount`, `collectCount` | engagement | (stored) |
| `videoMeta.duration` | seconds | (stored) |
| `createTimeISO`, `webVideoUrl` | timestamps / permalink | (stored) |

**Metric derivation (TikTok):**
- `follower_count = items[0].authorMeta.fans`
- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(items[i].playCount for i in 0..min(10, len(items)))`
- `video_count = items[0].authorMeta.video`

**One run per refresh.**

### 3c. Instagram — `apify/instagram-scraper`

The Instagram actor is a **multi-mode scraper**; what it returns depends on `resultsType`. For our use case we need two modes per refresh:

**Run 1 — profile details (follower count):**
```json
{
  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/<handle>/"],
  "resultsType": "details",
  "resultsLimit": 1,
  "addParentData": false
}
```

Output (one row, keyed on username):

| Field | Meaning | Maps to |

1	-- US-014 — RPC backing the auth-signup-lister edge function.
2	--
3	-- Contract: public.auth_signup_lister(p_username, p_email, p_org_name,
4	-- p_website_url) returns jsonb. On success: {"user_id": "<uuid>"}. On
5	-- conflict: {"error": "USERNAME_TAKEN" | "EMAIL_TAKEN"}. Any other
6	-- unique_violation is re-raised for the edge function to log as 500.
7	--
8	-- Atomicity: the function body runs in a single implicit transaction; a
9	-- failure on the lister_profiles insert rolls back the users insert too,
10	-- satisfying the "single transaction" acceptance criterion without
11	-- relying on PostgREST's per-call transactions.
12	--
13	-- Why this is in a migration: two separate supabase-js inserts are two
14	-- separate transactions, so a mid-flight crash would leave orphan users
15	-- rows. An RPC function gives us true atomicity for the signup flow.
16	--
17	-- Auth: revoked from anon/authenticated/public. Only service_role (used
18	-- by the edge function) may execute.
19	--
20	-- Spec gap handoff: the spec also requires per-IP rate limiting via a
21	-- signup_attempts table (§5.1) — that table does not exist yet and is
22	-- tracked as a future-story item in progress.txt Codebase Patterns. This
23	-- function is intentionally rate-limit-free for now.
24	
25	create or replace function public.auth_signup_lister(
26	  p_username extensions.citext,
27	  p_email extensions.citext,
28	  p_org_name text,
29	  p_website_url text default null
30	) returns jsonb
31	language plpgsql
32	set search_path = ''
33	as $$
34	declare
35	  v_user_id uuid;
36	  v_constraint text;
37	begin
38	  insert into public.users (role, username, email)
39	  values ('lister'::public.user_role, p_username, p_email)
40	  returning id into v_user_id;
41	
42	  insert into public.lister_profiles (user_id, org_name, website_url)
43	  values (v_user_id, p_org_name, p_website_url);
44	
45	  return jsonb_build_object('user_id', v_user_id);
46	exception
47	  when unique_violation then
48	    -- Constraint names below are Postgres's default for the inline `unique`
49	    -- declarations on public.users (username, email) in us_004. If US-004
50	    -- is ever rewritten to give those constraints explicit names, update
51	    -- both string literals here.
52	    get stacked diagnostics v_constraint = constraint_name;
53	    if v_constraint = 'users_username_key' then
54	      return jsonb_build_object('error', 'USERNAME_TAKEN');
55	    elsif v_constraint = 'users_email_key' then
56	      return jsonb_build_object('error', 'EMAIL_TAKEN');
57	    else
58	      raise;
59	    end if;
60	end;
61	$$;
62	
63	revoke all on function public.auth_signup_lister(
64	  extensions.citext, extensions.citext, text, text
65	) from public;
66	revoke all on function public.auth_signup_lister(
67	  extensions.citext, extensions.citext, text, text
68	) from anon, authenticated;
69	grant execute on function public.auth_signup_lister(
70	  extensions.citext, extensions.citext, text, text
71	) to service_role;
72	
73	comment on function public.auth_signup_lister(
74	  extensions.citext, extensions.citext, text, text
75	) is 'US-014 atomic signup RPC. Called only by the auth-signup-lister edge function via service_role. Returns jsonb {user_id} on success or {error: USERNAME_TAKEN|EMAIL_TAKEN}.';
76

1	-- US-007 follow-up #3: fix the previous requalification.
2	--
3	-- us_007_qualify_function_refs incorrectly schema-qualified `greatest()` and
4	-- `now()` as `pg_catalog.<fn>()`. `GREATEST` is a SQL parser construct, not a
5	-- callable function, so `pg_catalog.greatest(...)` raises 42883 at trigger
6	-- fire time. `now()` lives in pg_catalog and resolves bare even when the
7	-- user-set search_path is empty (pg_catalog is always implicitly searched).
8	--
9	-- This migration recreates both functions with bare `greatest()` / `now()`
10	-- and re-asserts `set search_path = ''` (CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION drops
11	-- prior SET clauses).
12	
13	create or replace function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
14	returns trigger
15	language plpgsql
16	set search_path = ''
17	as $$
18	declare p public.platform;
19	begin
20	  select sl.platform into p from public.social_links sl where sl.id = new.social_link_id;
21	  if p is null then
22	    raise exception 'social_link % not found', new.social_link_id;
23	  end if;
24	  if (p = 'tiktok'::public.platform
25	        and new.scrape_mode <> 'tiktok_profile'::public.scrape_mode)
26	  or (p = 'instagram'::public.platform
27	        and new.scrape_mode not in ('ig_details'::public.scrape_mode, 'ig_posts'::public.scrape_mode)) then
28	    raise exception 'scrape_mode % incoherent with platform %', new.scrape_mode, p;
29	  end if;
30	  return new;
31	end $$;
32	
33	create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
34	returns trigger
35	language plpgsql
36	security definer
37	set search_path = ''
38	as $$
39	declare
40	  sl       public.social_links%rowtype;
41	  lock_key bigint;
42	begin
43	  if new.status <> 'fresh'::public.metric_status then
44	    new.is_latest := false;
45	    return new;
46	  end if;
47	
48	  lock_key := pg_catalog.hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
49	  perform pg_catalog.pg_advisory_xact_lock(lock_key);
50	
51	  update public.metric_snapshots
52	     set is_latest = false
53	   where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
54	     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
55	     and is_latest;
56	
57	  new.is_latest := true;
58	
59	  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
60	
61	  if new.scrape_mode = 'tiktok_profile'::public.scrape_mode then
62	    update public.creator_profiles
63	       set tiktok_follower_count    = new.follower_count,
64	           tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
65	           tiktok_total_likes       = new.total_likes,
66	           tiktok_video_count       = new.video_count,
67	           tiktok_is_verified       = new.is_verified,
68	           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
69	           updated_at               = now()
70	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
71	
72	  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_details'::public.scrape_mode then
73	    update public.creator_profiles
74	       set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
75	           instagram_media_count    = new.video_count,
76	           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
77	           updated_at               = now()
78	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
79	
80	  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_posts'::public.scrape_mode then
81	    update public.creator_profiles
82	       set instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
83	           metrics_fetched_at          = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
84	           updated_at                  = now()
85	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
86	  end if;
87	
88	  return new;
89	end $$;
90

1	-- US-007: metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger.
2	--
3	-- Per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 6 (lines 564-597) and the trigger
4	-- definitions in §15 (lines 1205-1291).
5	--
6	-- Three pieces:
7	--   1. public.metric_snapshots — historized rows, one per Apify run.
8	--   2. app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence — BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE
9	--      trigger ensuring scrape_mode matches the linked social_link.platform.
10	--   3. app_private.denorm_metrics — BEFORE INSERT trigger that, for fresh
11	--      rows, demotes the previous latest under a (social_link, scrape_mode)
12	--      advisory lock and mirrors the owned columns into creator_profiles.
13	--
14	-- Spec gap (story text vs. spec DDL): the story acceptance criterion says the
15	-- advisory lock is keyed on (user_id, scrape_mode); the spec keys it on
16	-- (social_link_id, scrape_mode). The two are functionally equivalent because
17	-- social_links has a partial unique index on (user_id, platform). Following
18	-- the spec verbatim.
19	
20	-- =========================================================
21	-- 1. metric_snapshots table
22	-- =========================================================
23	create table public.metric_snapshots (
24	  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),

-- US-007: metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger.
--
-- Per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 6 (lines 564-597) and the trigger
-- definitions in §15 (lines 1205-1291).
--
-- Three pieces:
--   1. public.metric_snapshots — historized rows, one per Apify run.
--   2. app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence — BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE
--      trigger ensuring scrape_mode matches the linked social_link.platform.
--   3. app_private.denorm_metrics — BEFORE INSERT trigger that, for fresh
--      rows, demotes the previous latest under a (social_link, scrape_mode)
--      advisory lock and mirrors the owned columns into creator_profiles.
--
-- Spec gap (story text vs. spec DDL): the story acceptance criterion says the
-- advisory lock is keyed on (user_id, scrape_mode); the spec keys it on
-- (social_link_id, scrape_mode). The two are functionally equivalent because
-- social_links has a partial unique index on (user_id, platform). Following
-- the spec verbatim.

-- =========================================================
-- 1. metric_snapshots table
-- =========================================================
create table public.metric_snapshots (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  social_link_id    uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
  scrape_mode       scrape_mode not null,
  apify_run_id      text,
  status            metric_status not null,
  follower_count    integer,
  following_count   integer,
  total_likes       bigint,
  video_count       integer,
  avg_views_last_10 integer,
  is_verified       boolean,
  raw_payload       jsonb,
  fetched_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  is_latest         boolean not null default false,
  error_message     text
);

create index on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, fetched_at desc);

-- Only one "latest" snapshot per (social_link, scrape_mode); IG has at most two.
create unique index metric_snapshots_latest
  on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, scrape_mode) where is_latest;

-- Idempotency for webhook redeliveries — same Apify run never inserts twice.
create unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq
  on public.metric_snapshots (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null;

-- Janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` scans here. Partial keeps it sub-ms.
create index metric_snapshots_stuck_idx
  on public.metric_snapshots (fetched_at) where status = 'refreshing';

-- =========================================================
-- 2. Coherence trigger: scrape_mode ↔ social_link.platform
-- =========================================================
create or replace function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
returns trigger language plpgsql as $$
declare p platform;
begin
  select platform into p from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
  if p is null then
    raise exception 'social_link % not found', new.social_link_id;
  end if;
  if (p = 'tiktok'    and new.scrape_mode <> 'tiktok_profile')
  or (p = 'instagram' and new.scrape_mode not in ('ig_details', 'ig_posts')) then
    raise exception 'scrape_mode % incoherent with platform %', new.scrape_mode, p;
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_metric_snapshots_coherence
  before insert or update of scrape_mode, social_link_id on public.metric_snapshots
  for each row execute function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence();

-- =========================================================
-- 3. Race-free latest-row promotion + denormalization trigger
-- =========================================================
-- BEFORE INSERT so we can mutate new.is_latest; advisory lock serializes
-- concurrent webhook deliveries / manual refreshes for the same
-- (social_link, scrape_mode) pair.
create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
declare
  sl       public.social_links%rowtype;
  lock_key bigint;
begin
  if new.status <> 'fresh' then
    -- 'refreshing' and 'failed' rows never become latest, never denormalize.
    new.is_latest := false;
    return new;
  end if;

  lock_key := hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
  perform pg_advisory_xact_lock(lock_key);

  update public.metric_snapshots
     set is_latest = false
   where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
     and is_latest;

  new.is_latest := true;

  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;

  if new.scrape_mode = 'tiktok_profile' then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set tiktok_follower_count    = new.follower_count,
           tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
           tiktok_total_likes       = new.total_likes,
           tiktok_video_count       = new.video_count,
           tiktok_is_verified       = new.is_verified,
           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at               = now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;

  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_details' then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
           instagram_media_count    = new.video_count,
           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at               = now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;

  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_posts' then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
           metrics_fetched_at          = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at                  = now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;
  end if;

  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_denorm_metrics
  before insert on public.metric_snapshots
  for each row execute function app_private.denorm_metrics();

-- US-012 — Dev seed data per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7.
-- Creates: 1 lister, 1 active listing (v1 + pre/post conditions + sample video),
-- 1 creator with tiktok + instagram handles, and one metric_snapshots row for
-- each of the three scrape_modes (tiktok_profile, ig_details, ig_posts).
-- The app_private.denorm_metrics BEFORE-INSERT trigger (US-007) mirrors the
-- snapshots into public.creator_profiles, so no manual denorm updates here.
-- Fixed UUIDs so later stories (US-038 discover feed, US-055 edit campaign,
-- US-038+ verification flows) can reference the seeded rows deterministically.

do $$
declare
  v_lister_id  uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111001';
  v_creator_id uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111002';
  v_listing_id uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111010';
  v_version_id uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111011';
  v_tt_link_id uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111020';
  v_ig_link_id uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111021';
begin
  -- ---- lister ----------------------------------------------------------
  insert into public.users (id, role, username, email)
  values (v_lister_id, 'lister', 'acme_studio', 'lister@seed.marketify.test');

  insert into public.lister_profiles (user_id, org_name, website_url, description)
  values (
    v_lister_id, 'Acme Studio', 'https://acme.example',
    'Seed lister for development and RLS verification.'
  );

  -- ---- creator ---------------------------------------------------------
  insert into public.users (id, role, username, email)
  values (v_creator_id, 'creator', 'seed_creator', 'creator@seed.marketify.test');

  insert into public.creator_profiles (user_id, display_name, bio, country)
  values (v_creator_id, 'Seed Creator', 'Dev seed creator.', 'US');

  insert into public.social_links (
    id, user_id, platform, handle, status, handle_confirmed_at
  ) values
    (v_tt_link_id, v_creator_id, 'tiktok',    'seed_creator_tt', 'linked', now()),
    (v_ig_link_id, v_creator_id, 'instagram', 'seed_creator_ig', 'linked', now());

  -- ---- listing + v1 ----------------------------------------------------
  -- Insert as active immediately; current_version_id is wired after v1 exists.
  -- The follow-up UPDATE touches only current_version_id, so the US-010
  -- cascade trigger detects no versioned change and does not re-bump.
  insert into public.listings (
    id, lister_id, status,
    title, description, category,
    price_cents, currency, max_submissions,
    min_followers_tiktok, min_followers_instagram,
    published_at
  ) values (
    v_listing_id, v_lister_id, 'active',
    'Promote Acme Headphones',
    'Create a 30-60s short demonstrating our new headphones. See samples for vibe.',
    'tech',
    15000, 'USD', 10,
    1000, 500,
    now()
  );

  insert into public.listing_versions (
    id, listing_id, version_number,
    price_cents, currency, max_submissions,
    snapshot, changed_fields
  ) values (
    v_version_id, v_listing_id, 1,
    15000, 'USD', 10,
    jsonb_build_object(
      'title',           'Promote Acme Headphones',
      'price_cents',     15000,
      'currency',        'USD',
      'max_submissions', 10
    ),
    array[]::text[]
  );

  update public.listings
     set current_version_id = v_version_id
   where id = v_listing_id;

-- US-016 — RPC backing the apify-webhook edge function (tiktok_profile branch).
--
-- Contract: public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile(
--   p_run_id text, p_social_link_id uuid,
--   p_status public.metric_status, p_fetched_at timestamptz,
--   p_follower_count int, p_following_count int,
--   p_total_likes bigint, p_video_count int,
--   p_avg_views_last_10 int, p_is_verified boolean,
--   p_raw_payload jsonb, p_error_message text
-- ) returns jsonb:
--   {"inserted": true,  "duplicate": false, "snapshot_id": "<uuid>"}  on fresh insert
--   {"inserted": false, "duplicate": true}                              on ON CONFLICT no-op
--
-- Behavior: Inserts one public.metric_snapshots row keyed on apify_run_id.
--   The partial unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq (US-007) enforces
--   webhook-delivery idempotency: a second webhook carrying the same run_id
--   hits ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING and returns duplicate=true.
--
--   When a fresh 'fresh'-status row is inserted, the US-007 BEFORE INSERT
--   trigger trg_denorm_metrics fires — advisory-lock serialized — and mirrors
--   the tiktok_profile denorm columns to public.creator_profiles. When a
--   'failed'-status row is inserted, denorm_metrics short-circuits on
--   new.status <> 'fresh', sets is_latest=false, and skips the denorm
--   writes. That is the spec's design for retry-by-next-cron behavior.
--
-- Auth: revoked from anon / authenticated / public. Granted only to
--   service_role (the edge function's client).
--
-- Spec gap handoff — US-020 pre-refreshing rows:
--   US-020 (auth-signup-creator Apify dispatch) will pre-create metric_snapshots
--   rows in status='refreshing' before the webhook arrives, so the Apify run's
--   run_id is attached from dispatch time. This RPC does NOT currently detect
--   and transition a pre-existing 'refreshing' row — ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
--   would no-op and the row would stay 'refreshing' until the fail-stuck-
--   refreshing janitor cron (Codebase Pattern §16 spec gap) flips it to
--   'failed'. US-020 must extend either this RPC (to UPDATE-then-INSERT) or the
--   app_private.denorm_metrics trigger (to also fire BEFORE UPDATE) before
--   pre-refreshing rows ship. Tracked in progress.txt Codebase Patterns.

create or replace function public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile(
  p_run_id text,
  p_social_link_id uuid,
  p_status public.metric_status,
  p_fetched_at timestamptz,
  p_follower_count int,
  p_following_count int,
  p_total_likes bigint,
  p_video_count int,
  p_avg_views_last_10 int,
  p_is_verified boolean,
  p_raw_payload jsonb,
  p_error_message text
) returns jsonb
language plpgsql
set search_path = ''
as $$
declare
  v_snapshot_id uuid;
begin
  if p_run_id is null or length(p_run_id) = 0 then
    raise exception 'p_run_id must be non-empty';
  end if;
  if p_status not in ('fresh'::public.metric_status, 'failed'::public.metric_status) then
    raise exception 'p_status must be fresh or failed, got %', p_status;
  end if;

  insert into public.metric_snapshots (
    social_link_id,
    scrape_mode,
    apify_run_id,
    status,
    follower_count,
    following_count,
    total_likes,
    video_count,
    avg_views_last_10,
    is_verified,
    raw_payload,
    fetched_at,
    error_message
  ) values (
    p_social_link_id,
    'tiktok_profile'::public.scrape_mode,
    p_run_id,
    p_status,
    p_follower_count,
    p_following_count,
    p_total_likes,
    p_video_count,
    p_avg_views_last_10,
    p_is_verified,
    p_raw_payload,
    p_fetched_at,
    p_error_message
  )
  on conflict (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null
  do nothing
  returning id into v_snapshot_id;

  if v_snapshot_id is null then
    return jsonb_build_object('inserted', false, 'duplicate', true);
  end if;

  return jsonb_build_object(
    'inserted', true,
    'duplicate', false,
    'snapshot_id', v_snapshot_id
  );
end;
$$;

revoke all on function public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile(
  text, uuid, public.metric_status, timestamptz,
  int, int, bigint, int, int, boolean, jsonb, text
) from public;
revoke all on function public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile(
  text, uuid, public.metric_status, timestamptz,
  int, int, bigint, int, int, boolean, jsonb, text
) from anon, authenticated;
grant execute on function public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile(
  text, uuid, public.metric_status, timestamptz,
  int, int, bigint, int, int, boolean, jsonb, text
) to service_role;

comment on function public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile(
  text, uuid, public.metric_status, timestamptz,
  int, int, bigint, int, int, boolean, jsonb, text
) is 'US-016 webhook persistence RPC for tiktok_profile scrape_mode. Idempotent via partial unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq on apify_run_id. Returns jsonb {inserted, duplicate, snapshot_id?}.';
